mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, increasing the present value of future corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Twenty-day forward returns following below-median FEDFUNDS levels do not significantly exceed returns following above-median levels (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating household income expectations, reducing aggregate consumption and equity earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when the UNRATE trigger is activated over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer prices and a declining unemployment rate generate stagflationary wage-price dynamics that tighten real financial conditions and compress profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL momentum and UNRATE level does not significantly predict 60-day returns (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth signals robust consumer demand that supports corporate revenue expansion and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days only when the federal funds rate is below restrictive thresholds, because elevated rates override demand strength via higher discount rates.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS below 3%, positive RSAFS momentum fails to correlate with positive 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A steep contraction in industrial production below trend signals declining manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate output, leading to negative earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of trigger events over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate indicate an overheating labor market that triggers anticipatory pricing of restrictive monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal does not produce negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances during tightening cycles over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth above 2% annualized reduces recession probability and supports broad-based risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are not significantly higher after GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% than after growth below 2% (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 4% annualized forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, raising real discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 4% over a 3-year rolling window.
